commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an internal code-mode namespace registry and QuickJS bridge, plugin rollback cleanup, docs/tests, and a live Fictions benchmark harness.

PR surface: Source +535, Tests +479, Docs +44, Other +610. Total +1668 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR rather than a reported bug. The requested behavior is demonstrated by source tests plus the PR body's local and Crabbox live benchmark output.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Protected maintainer handling and the new security boundary require human approval; there is no narrow automated repair to queue.

target.title: feat: add internal code mode namespaces
target.body:
## Summary

- Add an internal code-mode namespace registry and QuickJS bridge so host-owned namespace globals can be exposed inside `exec` without adding model-visible tools.
- Gate namespaces by run catalog ownership, serialize only safe enumerable scope values, reject forged/unsafe paths, preserve namespace runtime across `wait`, and clear namespace registrations on plugin rollback.
- Document the internal namespace boundary and explicitly keep a public plugin SDK namespace API out of this PR until it can be loader-owned and cache-safe.
- Add a live OpenAI benchmark harness for a fake Fictions service and tighten the exec prompt so models know namespace calls must `return` a final value.

## Verification

- `fnm exec --using 24.15.0 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/code-mode.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (44 passed)
- `fnm exec --using 24.15.0 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.plugin-sdk.config.ts src/plugin-sdk/api-baseline.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (1 passed)
- `fnm exec --using 24.15.0 pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `env OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=0 fnm exec --using 24.15.0 pnpm check:changed`
- `fnm exec --using 24.15.0 pnpm build`
- `node --import tsx scripts/repro/code-mode-namespace-live.ts --tasks=3` (regular 3/3, code-namespace 3/3)
- `pnpm crabbox:run -- --allow-env OPENAI_API_KEY --idle-timeout 45m --ttl 90m --timing-json --shell -- 'node --import tsx scripts/repro/code-mode-namespace-live.ts --tasks=3'` (AWS Crabbox `cbx_6a216056aab1`, run `run_909ea916a62f`, regular 3/3, code-namespace 3/3)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` (90 passed)
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/code-mode.ts src/agents/code-mode.test.ts scripts/repro/code-mode-namespace-live.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (clean: no accepted/actionable findings)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: code mode can expose internal host-owned namespace globals inside QuickJS while keeping normal tools hidden behind `exec` and `wait`; live model runs can use that namespace surface to inspect and mutate a service.

Real environment tested: local macOS checkout with Node 24.15.0; AWS Crabbox c7a.8xlarge lease `cbx_6a216056aab1`; OpenAI Responses API with `gpt-5.4-mini` and a real `OPENAI_API_KEY` passed through Crabbox env forwarding.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: targeted code-mode Vitest, plugin SDK API baseline test, plugin SDK API check, local changed gate and build from the original namespace patch; after the live harness addition, ran the local and Crabbox Fictions benchmark, focused code-mode Vitest, targeted oxlint, whitespace check, and final autoreview as listed above.

Evidence after fix: local live benchmark passed 6/6 task-mode pairs: regular 3/3 with 10,758 ms, 9 model turns, 7 top-level tool calls, 7 service calls; code-namespace 3/3 with 17,248 ms, 9 model turns, 6 top-level tool calls, 4 service calls. Crabbox live benchmark passed 6/6 task-mode pairs: regular 3/3 with 12,228 ms, 9 model turns, 7 top-level tool calls, 7 service calls; code-namespace 3/3 with 7,728 ms, 9 model turns, 6 top-level tool calls, 4 service calls.

Observed result after fix: namespace globals are visible only when the owning plugin id has the required catalog tools in the run, same-named tools from other owners do not expose the namespace, forged namespace paths are rejected, namespace calls survive suspend/resume, and the live Fictions namespace completed risk audit, status promotion, and invoice aggregation tasks with fewer service calls than regular mode.

What was not tested: no real third-party plugin namespace integration, because this PR intentionally does not add a public plugin SDK namespace API. A repeat broad `pnpm check:changed` after the live harness commit did not complete because local `tsgolint` crashed with a Go SIGSEGV and a Blacksmith Testbox rerun stalled on its `Run Testbox` step; targeted lint/test/live proof passed after that.
target.author: steipete
